intro: Lotononis varia, also known as the "yellow lotononis" or "sweet pea", is a flowering plant species in the family Fabaceae, native to southern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of fever, wounds, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Lotononis varia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lotononis varia is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of a tincture or extract, often combined with other plants to enhance its effects.
